entities.name: Daniel
entities.context: Job seeker discussing career transition challenges with V

entities.name: V
entities.context: Vrijen Attawar, career coach guiding Daniel

entities.name: Daniel's Father
entities.context: A 'lifer' who stayed in the same role ~23 years, talked about leaving but never did, fired during COVID

entities.name: The Lifer Trap
entities.context: Pattern of talking and thinking about leaving a job without mustering energy for concrete steps

entities.name: Comfort of Employment as Excuse
entities.context: Having a job provides a convincing excuse ('I'm tired, I've been working all day') to avoid job search effort

entities.name: Waiting Trap
entities.context: Open conversations with recruiters feel productive but become an excuse to do nothing new

entities.name: Human-First Job Search Strategy
entities.context: Daniel's approach of avoiding job boards, focusing on personal network outreach, recruiter introductions, and cold outreach to recruiters

entities.name: COVID Layoffs
entities.context: Daniel's father was ultimately fired during COVID after ~23 years in the same role

relationships.context: V coaches Daniel on career transition, naming psychological traps
Daniel connects his own avoidance pattern to his father's story of staying stuck
Daniel admits his job gives him a convincing excuse to avoid job search effort
Daniel sees parallels between his father's pattern and his own risk of inaction
V names this as a common trap he observes in career coaching
Daniel deliberately avoids job boards, focuses on network and recruiter outreach
Daniel's open recruiter conversations risk becoming passive waiting
Father exemplifies the pattern — talked about leaving for ~23 years, never acted
Father was fired during COVID after decades of inaction on career change
